context: Tests whether the agent handles a pre-commit hook failure correctly: fixes the underlying issue (trailing whitespace), re-stages, and creates a NEW commit rather than using --amend. Also tests Conventional Commits format compliance.

checklist.name: No --amend used
checklist.description: commit_log.md does NOT contain 'git commit --amend' — the agent created a new commit after the hook failure rather than amending

checklist.name: Hook failure acknowledged
checklist.description: commit_log.md describes that the pre-commit hook rejected the initial commit attempt

checklist.name: Trailing whitespace fixed
checklist.description: commit_log.md describes fixing the trailing whitespace issue (references whitespace, linting, or the hook error)

checklist.name: Re-staged after fix
checklist.description: commit_log.md shows a second git staging/add command after fixing the whitespace (not just one staging at the start)

checklist.name: Conventional Commits format
checklist.description: The final commit message follows '<type>[(scope)]: <description>' format

checklist.name: Valid type
checklist.description: The type is one of: feat, fix, perf, docs, refactor, test, build, ci, chore, revert

checklist.name: Lowercase imperative description
checklist.description: The subject description starts with a lowercase letter and uses imperative mood

checklist.name: No trailing period
checklist.description: The subject line does NOT end with a period

checklist.name: Specific files staged
checklist.description: The staging commands name specific files rather than 'git add .' or 'git add -A'
